meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Israel issues new forced displacement orders in southern Lebanon
items.summary: Warning covers more than 10 villages and towns, including several in Nabatieh district that lie north of Litani River.

items.title: Gretchen Walsh lowers own 100m butterfly world record for fourth time
items.summary: Walsh won in 54.33 seconds at the Fort Lauderdale Open in Florida, where she set her previous world record of 54.60s.

items.title: The World Cup & Passport privilege
items.summary: With US President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies, some fans may never make it past the American border

items.title: Austrian police arrest man after discovery of rat poison in baby food
items.summary: Arrest of 39-year-old comes after Swiss company HiPP last month issued product recall over tainted baby food jars.

items.title: Japan’s Inoue beats Nakatani to retain undisputed super bantamweight title
items.summary: Naoya Inoue wins 116-112, 115-113, 116-112 in a fight billed as the biggest boxing match in ​Japanese history.

items.title: South Korea holds pro-Palestine protest in solidarity with Gaza
items.summary: Protesters in South Korea’s capital, Seoul, were in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza enduring genocidal attacks.

items.title: Man charged with murder of five-year-old Australian Indigenous girl
items.summary: The man was also charged with two other offences and is to appear ​in court in Darwin on Tuesday, police said.

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 65 as Trump reviews new plan to end war?
items.summary: Iran has sent the US a new 14-point proposal to end the war.

items.title: China blocks US sanctions against five ‘teapot’ refineries
items.summary: Ministry of Commerce says sanctions against refineries accused of importing Iranian oil violate international law.

items.title: Taiwan leader visits Eswatini despite China’s attempts to block trip
items.summary: President William Lai Ching-te says he met King Mswati III and signed trade agreements.

items.title: Joel Embiid, 76ers knock out second-seeded Celtics in NBA Playoffs
items.summary: The 76ers eliminate one of the favourites to book a second-round series against the New York Knicks.

items.title: In Yemen, Starlink internet brings opportunities – for some
items.summary: Despite Houthi resistance and affordability challenges, Starlink fuels growth in Yemen's digital workforce.

items.title: Teen F1 leader Antonelli takes Miami pole as race start time changed
items.summary: Threat of heavy rainstorm in Miami causes organisers to move up race start time to 17:00 GMT on Sunday.

items.title: Carlos Prates punishes Jack Della Maddalena at UFC Fight Night Australia
items.summary: Brazil's Prates crushes hometown hero Maddalena in the Perth fight; challenges the winner of Makhachev vs Garry.

items.title: Millions of jobs lost as Iranians battle ‘Operation Economic Fury’
items.summary: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is crippling life in Tehran.

items.title: US approves $8.6bn in arms sales to Middle East allies
items.summary: US State Department cites emergency circumstances to fast-track sales without the approval of Congress.

items.title: UAE lifts all air traffic restrictions introduced since Iran war
items.summary: UAE aviation authority's announcement is the latest sign of the travel sector's recovery across the Middle East.

items.title: Trump expresses doubt that Iran’s peace proposal is ‘acceptable’
items.summary: US president says he is considering the 14-point peace plan, but warns strikes could resume if Tehran 'misbehaves'.

items.title: Iran war live: Trump to review 14-point plan; Israel pounds Lebanon
items.summary: US president says he will 'soon be reviewing the plan Iran has just sent to us', but doesn't think he can make a deal.

items.title: Philippines volcano erupts, blanketing ash across the sky
items.summary: The Mayon Volcano has erupted, spewing a cloud of ash and debris across Bicol, in the Philippines.

items.title: The rise of political violence under Trump
items.summary: Redi Tlhabi speaks to Professor Robert Pape on the rise of political violence in the US.

items.title: Why are maritime laws failing to secure the seas?
items.summary: Wars and territorial disputes are rewriting the rules of global shipping.

items.title: Timmy the humpback whale escapes to the North Sea
items.summary: The whale calf's repeated stranding off the coast of Germany sparked widespread concern and extensive media coverage.

items.title: Spirit Airlines collapses amid rising fuel costs from war on Iran
items.summary: US budget carrier Spirit Airlines shuts down after talks for a government bailout failed.

items.title: At least six dead in heavy rains in northeast Brazil, thousands displaced
items.summary: Studies show extreme rain has become more frequent in country, as Pernambuco and Paraiba states again battered.
